explanations.3: Inside this test, we're replacing the real 'which' command with a fake one that always fails—this lets us test what happens when a binary isn't found.
explanations.16: The syntax ${2:-...} means 'use the second argument if it was provided; otherwise use this default message'.
explanations.18: This line checks if 'which' returns nothing (meaning the binary doesn't exist), and the '|| true' prevents the whole line from failing if 'which' errors out.
